hypothesis: When trained on USPTO-retro, retrosynthesis models will exhibit dataset bias such that high top-k and top-k round-trip accuracy on reactions with seen templates will not generalize to reactions with unseen templates, and template-free architectures (MEGAN, RootAligned) will show smaller performance drops than the template-based LocalRetro because template frequency and product–template similarity in the training data drive overfitting to seen disconnections.
